fix: repair squad-heartbeat.yml YAML — cron keyword split across lines

The word 'cron' in the schedule trigger was split into 'cr' on one line
and 'on:' on the next, causing YAML to interpret it as a second top-level
'on:' key. This made GitHub Actions fail to parse the schedule correctly.

Also audited squad-board-sync.yml, squad-issue-assign.yml, and
squad-triage.yml — no similar issues found.

commit.message:
fix(simulator): use weighted vehicle type distribution in default mode (#93)

* fix(simulator): use weighted vehicle type distribution in default mode

When the simulator starts without a scenario, all vehicles were created
as type 'car'. This meant trucks, buses, motorcycles, and ambulances
never appeared unless a scenario explicitly spawned them.

Changes:
- Add DEFAULT_VEHICLE_TYPE_WEIGHTS (60% car, 15% truck, 12% motorcycle,
  10% bus, 3% ambulance) and distributeByWeight() utility
- VehicleRegistry.loadFromData() now uses weighted distribution when no
  explicit types are provided instead of hardcoding 'car'
- Vehicle names now reflect their type (e.g. Car-1, Truck-2, Bus-3)
- Add VEHICLE_TYPES env var for custom JSON distribution override
- VehicleManager passes config.vehicleTypes through to registry
- Add vehicleProfiles.test.ts with distribution unit tests
- Update existing tests to expect mixed types instead of all-car

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ui): add client-side vehicle type filtering to VehiclesLayer

The type filter legend sent subscribe filters to the server but never
filtered vehicles client-side. Vehicles already in the store remained
visible after toggling a type off, because VehiclesLayer only checked
hiddenFleetIds, not hiddenVehicleTypes.

Changes:
- Thread hiddenVehicleTypes prop: App → Map → VehiclesLayer
- Add type check in VehiclesLayer render loop alongside fleet check
- Add test: 'skips vehicles of hidden types'
- Fix existing test defaultProps to include hiddenVehicleTypes

fix: codebase quality audit — 26 issues across all apps (#94)

* fix: codebase quality audit — 26 issues across all apps

Systematic audit of simulator, adapter, UI, shared packages, and config
found and fixed 26 issues covering bugs, validation gaps, inconsistencies,
and bad patterns.

Simulator (7 fixes):
- Path traversal vulnerability in replay file parameter
- Missing validation that edge IDs exist in network for incidents
- Unbounded array length in direction requests (max 100)
- Race condition in concurrent replay start (mutex guard)
- Silent promise rejections in pathfinding (added logging)
- Graceful shutdown hang on uncaught exceptions (hard timeout)
- Inconsistent error handling patterns across routes

Adapter (8 fixes):
- Missing error handling in SIGTERM shutdown
- Silent data loss in all sink plugins when disconnected
- Missing VehicleUpdate field validation on /sync endpoint
- Race condition in Express server startup before plugins ready
- Missing input validation in config endpoints
- Memory leak in Redpanda health check Promise.race
- Unsafe GraphQL query interpolation from config
- Inconsistent error response format across endpoints

UI (7 fixes):
- Missing event listener cleanup in multiple hooks (memory leak)
- WebSocket handler overwrites (changed to Set-based multi-listener)
- WebSocket handler errors crash all message processing (try-catch)
- Missing timer cleanup in useOptions on unmount
- Incomplete ResizeObserver cleanup (added disconnect)
- Race condition in useClock between promise and listener
- Static random interval in incident auto-generation

Shared/Config (4 fixes):
- Coordinate order inconsistency between Position and GeoFence docs
- Inconsistent script naming breaks turbo type-check task
- Missing eslint-config and lint/format scripts in network package
- Docker compose environment variable inconsistency

commit.message:
Add transaction history blocks to NFC card: anti-tamper checksum, atomic writes, last-4 display (#23)

* Initial plan

* Implement transaction history data blocks for NFC card

- Add TransactionBlock.kt: new data class with 32-byte layout (2 blocks)
  spanning initTimestamp (4B), up to 4 tx entries (6B each: 3B seconds
  offset, 1B op, 2B amount), and 4-byte HMAC-SHA256 checksum keyed with
  PSK+UID for anti-tamper detection.
- Update MainActivity to read/write all 3 data blocks; verify checksum
  before every add/deduct (refuse with error on tamper); retry interrupted
  writes using in-memory PendingWrite state; initialize transaction blocks
  with timestamp on format; display last 4 transactions after card read.
- Add transaction history section to activity_main.xml (gone by default).
- Add new string resources for transaction display and error messages.

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/f5b9270f-88fe-4b32-9a2e-f25468f92a31

commit.message:
fix: prevent PyQt6 abort() on unhandled exceptions in EditActorDialog slots

Three root causes addressed:

1. eventFilter AttributeError during construction
   - description_input, biography_input, gmnotes_input are assigned sequentially
   - If a focus event fires between assignments, accessing an unset attribute
     raises AttributeError which PyQt6 turns into abort()
   - Fix: use getattr(self, 'biography_input', None) etc. + try/except wrapper

2. _rich_text_value false-positive on macOS dark mode
   - Qt injects 'background-color:' into the default <body> CSS in dark mode,
     so the old check always returned the full HTML blob unnecessarily
   - Fix: search for inline formatting elements (<span>, <i>, <u>) in body
     content only, not document-level CSS

3. Slot methods lacked exception guards
   - Any Python exception escaping a PyQt6 slot calls qFatal() -> abort()
   - Wrapped _on_save, _update_preview, _apply_italic, _apply_underline,
     _apply_highlight, _clear_format, and eventFilter in try/except that
     logs and recovers instead of crashing

Also added sys.excepthook override in gui_main.py to log the actual Python
traceback before abort fires, making future slot errors diagnosable.

commit.message:
Fix spectrum/moves parsing for Reporter-style cards

Three parser fixes:

1. OCR bullet normalization: Tesseract renders '•' as standalone 'e'
   or 'o'. Add _normalize_ocr_bullets() in danger_transform.py — detects
   when 3+ lines start with '^e/o + space + non-space' (safe heuristic
   that doesn't fire on normal prose words) and replaces with proper '•'.
   This fixes the 0-moves result for OCR'd text and ensures continuation
   lines are correctly merged into their bullet.

2. Soft-hyphen line merging: _normalize_line_breaks now strips a trailing
   '-' from a bullet's current fragment before joining a continuation
   line, so 'interview-\ning them' becomes 'interviewing them'.

3. Spectrum title-case detection: 'Fool 3 / Scare 4' (common from PDF
   copy-paste) was silently ignored because _SPECTRUM_ALLCAPS_RE requires
   ALL-CAPS names. New _SPECTRUM_TITLECASE_RE handles slash-separated
   spectrum lines where names are title-case or mixed-case. Also wired
   into _parse_spectrum_line so the names/tiers are extracted correctly.

4. Hard move text detection: 'Inquisitive: As a hard move, ...' was
   classified as CUSTOM. Now also detects 'as a hard/soft move' in
   description prose (not just inside parentheses).

Adds 6 regression tests for the Reporter card in both clean and OCR form.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
